layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: FastAPI entry point, lifespan management, route handlers (chat, askfer, ingest), request/response schemas, and authentication dependencies.

layers.name: Agent & Pipeline Layer
layers.description: LangGraph pipelines (general chat + askfer portfolio), intent classification rules, LLM client factory, system prompts, and short- and long-term memory services.

layers.name: Data & Retrieval Layer
layers.description: Postgres / Redis / Qdrant client factories, SQLAlchemy ORM models, and the hybrid vector+lexical retriever with reranking.

layers.name: Ingestion Layer
layers.description: Content ingestion pipelines that load external knowledge into the RAG store: generic document pipeline, Moodle course scraper, and portfolio homepage/knowledge synchronizer.

layers.name: Evaluation & Testing
layers.description: Evaluation harness (runner, judge, tasks), eval baseline/results JSON, golden test set, and harassment-rewrite guard unit test.

layers.name: Utility & Configuration
layers.description: Cross-cutting helpers (semantic cache, language detection, batched logger, token counter), pydantic-settings configuration, embedding config, and logging setup.

layers.name: Personal Knowledge Base
layers.description: Markdown corpus (CV, profile, homepage, instructional-design knowledge articles, project portfolio entries) that feeds the askfer portfolio RAG index.

layers.name: Project Documentation & Tooling
layers.description: Engineering design docs, feature specifications, top-level README, and standalone developer scripts (token measurement, smoke tests) that are not part of the main runtime.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Container build, multi-service Compose stack, and per-project ignore configuration.

layers.name: Frontend Assets
layers.description: Static HTML, CSS, and JavaScript served by FastAPI for the chat widget, the index page, and the Moodle ingestion admin UI, plus the suggested-question chips data.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the project root to understand what ai-lms-agent is. Despite the empty README, the design spec under docs/superpowers/specs/ describes the askfer portfolio chat feature that anchors the project: a personal portfolio RAG assistant backed by LangGraph, Qdrant, and Postgres.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: app/main.py is the FastAPI application factory. The lifespan context manager boots Postgres, Redis, Qdrant, and Phoenix observability before yielding, and tears them down on shutdown. This single file wires the API routes, static assets, and middleware together — it is the best single-file summary of the runtime topology.

tour.title: API Routes and Schemas
tour.description: The app/api/routes/ package contains the three HTTP entry points: chat (general conversational streaming), askfer (portfolio-grounded RAG), and ingest (URL/document triggers). Pydantic schemas in app/api/schemas.py define the wire format, while app/api/auth.py supplies JWT/API-key auth dependencies.

tour.title: LangGraph Chat Pipelines
tour.description: app/graph/pipeline.py is the core LangGraph state machine that orchestrates retrieval-augmented generation, intent routing, and LLM calls for the main chat. The askfer-specific pipeline (askfer_pipeline.py) layers greeting, off-scope, and malicious-input handlers on top, then dispatches into a vector-retrieval node and an answer-generation node.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph state machines are built with a typed state dict (see app/graph/state.py) that flows through conditional edges. Each node is a pure function (state, config) -> partial state, which makes them composable and easy to instrument with OpenTelemetry.

tour.title: LLM Client and Prompts
tour.description: app/llm/client.py centralizes the LLM client factory — wrapping OpenAI-compatible providers with retries, token accounting, and Phoenix instrumentation. The prompts modules separate the general chat system prompt (prompts.py) from the askfer portfolio-tuned prompt (askfer_prompts.py).

tour.title: Hybrid Retrieval
tour.description: app/retrieval/hybrid_retriever.py fuses Qdrant vector search with Postgres lexical search and an optional reranker to ground LLM answers in the personal knowledge base. This module is the bridge between the LLM layer and the data layer; the schemas module defines the retrieval output contract.

tour.title: Data Layer
tour.description: app/database/ holds the three runtime stores: Postgres (async SQLAlchemy, models in models.py), Redis (caching and rate limiting), and Qdrant (vector store for long-term memory and RAG). The SQLAlchemy models define users, conversations, courses, and message history that the API and LangGraph pipelines read from and write to.

tour.title: Memory Services
tour.description: Short-term conversation memory (app/agents/memory.py) maintains append/summarize/history functions plus follow-up extraction. Long-term memory (long_term_memory_qdrant.py) persists user episodes and preferences into Qdrant so the assistant can remember context across sessions.

tour.title: Content Ingestion
tour.description: app/ingestion/ contains three loaders. portfolio_sync.py pulls the personal homepage and knowledge-base markdown into the RAG index. moodle_sync.py scrapes Moodle course material. pipeline.py is a generic document pipeline that handles loaders, chunking, embedding, and Qdrant upsert.

tour.title: Personal Knowledge Base
tour.description: data/personal/ is the source corpus that the askfer pipeline retrieves against. It is a curated set of Markdown documents covering the user's CV, profile, instructional-design frameworks (ADDIE, Bloom's, andragogy, Kirkpatrick), tools, and project portfolio entries — the raw material for portfolio-grounded answers.

tour.title: Configuration and Utilities
tour.description: app/config/settings.py centralizes pydantic-settings-based configuration (environment variables, feature flags). app/utils/ holds cross-cutting helpers: a Redis-backed semantic cache for LLM responses (cache.py), language detection (lang.py), batched structured logging (logger_batch.py), and tiktoken-based token counting (token_counter.py).

tour.title: Background Workers and Evaluation
tour.description: app/worker.py is the ARQ entry point for long-running ingestion and evaluation jobs. app/eval/ contains the evaluation harness — runner.py executes the golden set, judge.py uses an LLM-as-judge for open-ended scoring, and tasks.py defines the task suite. data/eval/ holds the JSONL golden set and per-run results.

tour.title: Containerization and Deployment
tour.description: Dockerfile builds the FastAPI app image and docker-compose.yml orchestrates the full local stack — app, ARQ worker, Qdrant, Postgres, Redis, and Phoenix — for development and demo. The .env.example documents every configuration variable consumed by the runtime.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds (builder + runtime) keep production images lean by separating compilation dependencies from runtime libraries. Docker Compose's `depends_on` plus healthchecks orchestrates service startup order so the API never boots before its data stores are ready.

tour.title: Frontend Widget
tour.description: app/static/ contains the static HTML, CSS, and JavaScript that FastAPI serves: the askfer chat widget (askfer.html + script.js), the index landing page, and the Moodle ingestion admin UI (moodle_ingest.html). askfer-chips.json supplies the suggested follow-up questions shown in the chat UI.
